description_long: This is a bulbous ceramic vase with a single curved handle attached to its neck, featuring a narrow mouth and a slightly flared rim. The surface is predominantly white with reddish-brown horizontal striations and weathered patches, giving it a rustic, aged appearance. Around the shoulder of the vase, there are hand-painted decorative motifs in orange, green, and brown, resembling stylized leaves or petals. The handle is robust and smoothly integrated into the body, suggesting both decorative and functional intent. The base is small and circular, providing stable support for upright placement. The overall finish is matte with visible brushwork and some surface irregularities, enhancing its artisanal character.
description: A rustic, bulbous ceramic vase with a single handle, decorated with orange and green painted motifs and horizontal striations on a white, weathered surface.
description_view_0: This view highlights the handle and the painted motifs on the shoulder, with the mouth and rim clearly visible.
description_view_1: The back view shows the full rounded body and the uninterrupted painted pattern, with the handle not visible.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, this angle shows the handle from the opposite side, emphasizing the symmetry and painted details.
description_view_3: The front view centers the neck and mouth, displaying the painted motifs symmetrically and the handle just out of sight.
